headline: Western Digital (WDC) Stock Slides As AI Memory Selloff Hits Chipmakers
summary: Global memory and AI infrastructure stocks, including Western Digital (NasdaqGS:WDC), saw a sharp selloff after a downturn in Korean memory companies. The pullback followed a period of record highs tied to AI driven demand, supply tightness, and strong risk appetite for chipmakers. The move reflects wider technology sector concerns, including valuation questions, regulatory changes, and market liquidations in South Korea. Western Digital shares were caught in the broader shift in sentiment...

headline: Wall Street Says AI Is Slowing. Taiwan Semiconductor’s Latest Move Says Otherwise.
summary: Despite a sharp AI stock selloff, TSM's reported 5% to 10% price hike on advanced chip production suggests demand remains strong. Companies don't pay more for scarce capacity if growth is fading.

content.title: Wall Street Says AI Is Slowing. Taiwan Semiconductor’s Latest Move Says Otherwise.
content.summary: Despite a sharp AI stock selloff, TSM's reported 5% to 10% price hike on advanced chip production suggests demand remains strong. Companies don't pay more for scarce capacity if growth is fading.

headline: Chip stocks surge after-hours on Micron’s record quarter
summary: Investing.com - Micron Technology Inc (NASDAQ:MU) delivered the strongest quarterly results in its history after Wednesday's close, posting fiscal third-quarter 2026 revenue of $41.46 billion against a Wall Street consensus of $35.69 billion, a 16.2% beat, and earnings per share of $25.11 versus the $20.49 estimate, a 22.6% upside surprise. The print sent Micron shares up roughly 12.6% in extended trade to around $1,180.97, recovering from a regular-session close of $1,047.20.

content.title: Chip stocks surge after-hours on Micron’s record quarter
content.summary: Investing.com - Micron Technology Inc (NASDAQ:MU) delivered the strongest quarterly results in its history after Wednesday's close, posting fiscal third-quarter 2026 revenue of $41.46 billion against a Wall Street consensus of $35.69 billion, a 16.2% beat, and earnings per share of $25.11 versus the $20.49 estimate, a 22.6% upside surprise. The print sent Micron shares up roughly 12.6% in extended trade to around $1,180.97, recovering from a regular-session close of $1,047.20.

headline: Micron and Qualcomm forecasts ignite $400 billion AI chip stock rally
summary: Shares of chipmakers surged late on Wednesday, adding over $400 billion in market ‌value after strong forecasts from Micron Technology and Qualcomm ‌breathed fresh life into Wall Street's recently waning AI stock rally. Micron surged ​12% in extended trade after forecasting quarterly earnings above analysts' estimates, signaling that heavy investments in AI-related infrastructure will drive strong demand for its memory chips. Also after the bell, Qualcomm ‌said it expects $15 billion ⁠in sales from its data center business by 2029 as it moves beyond its core smartphone ⁠chip business and shifts its focus to AI.
